page: Amazon Web Services a1.medium is an ARM64-based instance powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features a single dedicated physical core utilizing the ARMv8 Cortex-A72 architecture running at 2.3 GHz with one thread and no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tier of modern cloud hardware. While stress-ng single-core CPU performance sits in the average range at 1449.88, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the bottom 10%. Memory read-only bandwidth for large blocks is 10.43 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is 5.95 GB/sec. Geekbench scores reflect this entry-level performance, with a single-core score of 386.0 and a multi-core score of 384.0. Cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated AES, but the processor lacks advanced vector or matrix extensions like AVX or AMX.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the a1.medium offers a budget-friendly entry point into ARM64 development and testing without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances, thanks to its dedicated core allocation. However, the low memory capacity and modest processing power present clear tradeoffs. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, simple microservices, low-traffic Redis caching, and compiling or testing software targeting the ARM64 architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.medium is an entry-level, ARM64-based instance powered by a first-generation AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processor. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features one dedicated physical core clocked at 2.3 GHz, 2.0 GB of memory, and no local storage. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU and memory performance in the bottom ten percent, though its single-core stress-ng performance is average. Lacking advanced vector extensions but supporting AES cryptography, this instance offers a cost-efficient, dedicated-core option for ARM64 development. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, and simple microservices. It provides a predictable performance profile without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances, making it a stable choice for testing environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.medium is an ARM64 virtual server featuring a 1-core AWS Graviton processor, 2.0 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, designed for entry-level ARM workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.medium is an ARM64 instance with 1 dedicated Graviton core and 2.0 GB of RAM, ideal for basic web serving and ARM testing.
tagline: An entry level single core ARM64 cloud server designed for lightweight microservices basic web serving and software development testing
bullet_points: Powered by a first-generation AWS Graviton ARM64 processor at 2.3 GHz
Features 1 dedicated physical core with 2.0 GB of system memory
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
Suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and ARM64 testing
